meaning: v.包,蒙住,压抑(声音)n.围巾,头巾,消声器
examples: He tried to muffle the alarm clock by putting it under his pillow.
Blake held his handkerchief over the mouthpiece to muffle his voice.
In reality, some people are vocal and may moan and groan, other folks may muffle any sounds with a pillow, and yet others may not make a single peep.
Other options include sound equalizers, similar to those on MP3 players, and sound-cancellation features, similar to headsets that muffle annoying noise.
It aims to muffle the gene for a protein called apoliprotein B, or ApoB, that carries fatty cholesterol to the arteries, creating thick plaques.
